attack_chain.description: Attacker registers or implicitly acquires the Amplify subdomain master.d19tgz4vpyd5.amplifyapp.com as a staging and callback endpoint. AMBERSQUID obtains this domain automatically when creating Amplify apps in victim accounts. Documented only — no emulation code implements this step.

implementation.method: Documented only. No API calls. Attacker's Amplify subdomain is a side-effect of T1059.009 CreateApp calls made in victim accounts during prior campaigns.
implementation.details: attack.py logs 'DOCUMENTED [T1583.001]: Attacker domain master.d19tgz4vpyd5.amplifyapp.com pre-acquired via prior Amplify app creation in separate victim accounts. No action required.' and continues.

cleanup_actions: No cleanup required — documented only, no resources created
iocs_generated: Domain: master.d19tgz4vpyd5.amplifyapp.com

attack_chain.description: Attacker stages a malicious Docker image containing SRBMiner-MULTI (UPX-packed) on a public or attacker-controlled registry. The image entrypoint (entrypoint.sh) reads injected AWS credentials from environment variables and executes a suite of deployment scripts. Simulated — the benign ubuntu:22.04 image in ambersquid-task-definition stands in.

implementation.method: Simulated. attack.py logs 'SIMULATED [T1608.001]: Would push malicious Docker image containing SRBMiner-MULTI (UPX-packed) with scripts: entrypoint.sh, amplify-role.sh, repo.sh, code.sh, jalan.sh, sup0.sh, ecs.sh, ulang.sh, note.sh, salah.sh, delete.sh, stoptrigger.sh, scale.sh, restart.sh, amplify.yml, index.py, amplify-role.json, sugo.json, ecsTaskExecutionRole.json. Benign ubuntu:22.04 image used as stand-in.' and continues.
implementation.details: Real image executes SRBMiner-MULTI targeting ZEPHYR, TRX, RVN, XMR pools. entrypoint.sh calls amplify-role.sh (IAM role creation), repo.sh (CodeCommit provisioning), code.sh (CodeBuild), note.sh (SageMaker), ecs.sh (Fargate), scale.sh (Auto Scaling).

cleanup_actions: No cleanup required — simulated only, no resources created
iocs_generated: Tool: SRBMiner-MULTI (UPX-packed)
Tool: Docker
Files: entrypoint.sh, amplify-role.sh, repo.sh, code.sh, jalan.sh, sup0.sh, ecs.sh, ulang.sh, note.sh, salah.sh, delete.sh, stoptrigger.sh, scale.sh, restart.sh, amplify.yml, index.py

attack_chain.description: Victim's ECS task executes the malicious container (ubuntu:22.04 stand-in for staged miner image). The container receives victim IAM credentials as plaintext environment variables AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID and AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY. attack.py simulates the credential harvest by reading these values from Pulumi stack outputs (or env vars AWS_VICTIM_ACCESS_KEY_ID / AWS_VICTIM_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY) and constructs the victim_creds boto3 session.

implementation.method: attack.py reads victim credentials from Pulumi stack outputs via subprocess call, then calls boto3 ECS RunTask to start the container and describe_tasks to confirm it is running. Victim boto3 session is constructed from extracted credentials.
implementation.simulation_note: Container runs 'sleep infinity' — benign stand-in for SRBMiner-MULTI. No mining pool connections. Mining pool ports blocked by ambersquid-task-sg.

cleanup_actions: ecs:StopTask on running task ARN before pulumi destroy
iocs_generated: ECS task launched from ubuntu:22.04 with AWS credential env vars injected as plaintext
Container command: sleep infinity (miner stand-in)

attack_chain.description: Attacker validates stolen victim credentials via STS GetCallerIdentity to confirm account ID, user ARN, and region. Enumerates the caller's IAM permissions to assess attack surface. Establishes the victim_creds boto3 session used as the root credential for all subsequent phases.

implementation.method: boto3 STS and IAM calls using victim_creds session.

cleanup_actions: Read-only — no cleanup needed
iocs_generated: GetCallerIdentity from non-console IAM user session
Rapid policy enumeration immediately after credential harvest

attack_chain.description: Attacker creates three IAM roles using victim credentials, replicating AMBERSQUID's precise role set: (1) AWSCodeCommit-Role with Amplify, CodeCommit, and CloudWatch full access for multi-region code staging; (2) sugo-role with SageMaker full access for notebook-based mining; (3) ecsTaskExecutionRole (attacker-created) with AdministratorAccess and ECS full access for unrestricted container deployment. Trust policies allow the victim account root and ECS service to assume each role.

implementation.method: boto3 IAM CreateRole then AttachRolePolicy calls using victim_creds session. Three roles created sequentially.

cleanup_actions: iam:DetachRolePolicy then iam:DeleteRole AWSCodeCommit-Role
iam:DetachRolePolicy then iam:DeleteRole sugo-role
iam:DetachRolePolicy then iam:DeleteRole ecsTaskExecutionRole (attacker-created — NOT the Pulumi-managed ambersquid-ecs-execution-role)
iocs_generated: IAM role created: AWSCodeCommit-Role
IAM role created: sugo-role
IAM role created: ecsTaskExecutionRole with AdministratorAccess — critical detection signal
AdministratorAccess policy attached to attacker-created role from non-console session

attack_chain.description: Attacker assumes all three attacker-created IAM roles via STS AssumeRole, generating independent STS session tokens. These sessions persist even if the original victim IAM key is rotated or revoked. Establishes codecommit_role_session (Amplify/CodeCommit/CodeBuild), sugo_role_session (SageMaker), and ecs_exec_role_session (ECS + AdminAccess) used throughout subsequent phases.

implementation.method: boto3 STS AssumeRole calls using victim_creds session. All three sessions constructed and stored in attack.py state.

cleanup_actions: STS session tokens expire automatically after 3600 seconds — no manual cleanup required
iocs_generated: AssumeRole to AWSCodeCommit-Role from victim IAM user (non-console)
AssumeRole to sugo-role from victim IAM user
AssumeRole to ecsTaskExecutionRole (AdministratorAccess) from victim IAM user
Three parallel STS role sessions created within seconds from single IAM user

attack_chain.technique_name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: Cloud API
attack_chain.description: Attacker uses the three role sessions to burst-provision miner infrastructure across multiple AWS services in us-east-1: CodeCommit repo in us-west-2 (us-east-1 already exists via Pulumi), one Amplify app, one CodeBuild project, ECS cluster and task definition, and one SageMaker notebook instance. In real AMBERSQUID: 5 Amplify apps per region across 8 regions, 3 CodeBuild projects per region, 8 SageMaker ml.t3.2xlarge notebooks across 4 regions, and up to 30 Fargate tasks across 16 regions.

implementation.method: boto3 calls using codecommit_role_session (Amplify, CodeCommit, CodeBuild), sugo_role_session (SageMaker), and ecs_exec_role_session (ECS). Each service provisioned sequentially.

api_calls.note: us-east-1 repo already created by Pulumi (ambersquid-codecommit-repo). Expect RepositoryNameExistsException if region already has repo — log and continue.

args.name: miner-app
args.buildSpec:
version: 0.1
frontend:
  phases:
    build:
      commands:
        - echo mining

api_calls.note: ml.t3.medium (not ml.t3.2xlarge) to minimize cost. Stop immediately after confirming Pending state.

cleanup_actions: amplify:DeleteApp miner-app
codebuild:DeleteProject miner-build-small
ecs:DeleteCluster miner-cluster (after deregistering task definitions)
ecs:DeregisterTaskDefinition miner-task all revisions
sagemaker:StopNotebookInstance miner-notebook then sagemaker:DeleteNotebookInstance miner-notebook
codecommit:DeleteRepository test in us-west-2 (if successfully created)
iocs_generated: Amplify app created with CodeCommit source using attacker role
CodeBuild project targeting private CodeCommit repo from non-console session
ECS cluster miner-cluster created outside Pulumi-managed resources
SageMaker notebook created with attacker-controlled sugo-role
Multi-service provisioning burst from single IAM session within minutes

attack_chain.description: Attacker accesses CodeCommit repositories and would push miner binaries (SRBMiner-MULTI) and deployment scripts as Git commits, implanting the malicious image source inside the victim's internal code infrastructure. Simulated — repositories are accessed but no malicious content is pushed per operational notes.

implementation.method: boto3 CodeCommit GetRepository call using codecommit_role_session to verify access. No PutFile or git push executed.

api_calls.note: Verifies codecommit_role_session can access the Pulumi-created repo. Confirms AMBERSQUID would have push access.

implementation.simulation_note: SIMULATED: attack.py logs 'SIMULATED [T1525]: Would git push entrypoint.sh, amplify-role.sh, repo.sh, code.sh, jalan.sh, sup0.sh, ecs.sh, ulang.sh, note.sh, salah.sh, delete.sh, stoptrigger.sh, scale.sh, restart.sh, amplify.yml, index.py, amplify-role.json, sugo.json, ecsTaskExecutionRole.json to CodeCommit repos across up to 16 regions. Skipping to avoid malicious content staging.' No content pushed.

cleanup_actions: GetRepository is read-only — no cleanup needed
iocs_generated: CodeCommit GetRepository access using attacker-assumed AWSCodeCommit-Role session
SIMULATED: Multi-region CodeCommit repo push with miner deployment scripts

attack_chain.description: Attacker performs broad AWS infrastructure discovery using victim credentials: enumerating all enabled regions, IAM users and roles, S3 buckets (discovering the prod-infra-terraform-state honeypot), retrieving the fake terraform.tfstate (triggering canary alert), listing SecretsManager secrets, and retrieving the prod/database/master_credentials canary secret. Surfaces the honey IAM user (prod-deploy-svc) for potential lateral credential harvesting.

implementation.method: boto3 EC2, IAM, S3, STS, and SecretsManager calls using victim_creds session.

api_calls.note: Retrieves decoy tfstate containing canary AKIA credentials for prod-deploy-svc honey IAM user. Triggers canary alert if monitored.
Retrieves canary secret with fake RDS credentials — surfaces in CloudTrail as high-fidelity IOC.

cleanup_actions: Read-only — no cleanup needed
iocs_generated: ListBuckets followed immediately by GetObject on terraform.tfstate — canary triggered
GetSecretValue on prod/database/master_credentials canary
Rapid IAM enumeration (ListRoles, ListUsers, GetAccountSummary) from non-console session
Discovery of ambersquid-honey-iam-user (prod-deploy-svc) with active key

attack_chain.description: Attacker registers an ECS Fargate task definition for the miner container using the ecsTaskExecutionRole (AdministratorAccess). Simulated — task definition registered only, no ECS service or RunTask call made because launching Fargate services with desiredCount=30 across 16 regions creates massive compute charges per operational notes.

implementation.method: boto3 ECS RegisterTaskDefinition using ecs_exec_role_session. No CreateService call.
implementation.simulation_note: SIMULATED: attack.py logs 'SIMULATED [T1610]: Would call ecs:CreateService with desiredCount=30, launchType=FARGATE for miner-fargate-task across 16 regions. Skipping to avoid massive Fargate charges.' Task definition registered only for CloudTrail visibility.

cleanup_actions: ecs:DeregisterTaskDefinition miner-fargate-task all revisions
iocs_generated: ECS task definition registered with AdministratorAccess executionRole (ecsTaskExecutionRole — attacker-created)
SIMULATED: 30-task Fargate service creation across 16 regions

attack_chain.technique_name: Modify Cloud Compute Infrastructure: Create Cloud Instance
attack_chain.description: Attacker provisions large-scale compute for mining: EC2 Auto Scaling groups (on-demand 'task' and spot 'task1', 8 instances each across 4 regions), CloudFormation stacks with EC2 ImageBuilder pipelines (cron every minute), and 8x SageMaker ml.t3.2xlarge notebooks across 4 regions. Simulated — describe and validate calls only, no compute launched per operational notes (immediate AWS charges).

implementation.method: boto3 EC2, CloudFormation, and Auto Scaling describe/validate dry-run calls using victim_creds. No resource creation.

attack_chain.description: Attacker disables CloudTrail logging to blind defenders and deletes recent log objects from the S3 bucket. Uses victim_creds for CloudTrail StopLogging and S3 DeleteObject. Uses codecommit_role_session to demonstrate AMBERSQUID's CodeCommit repo deletion as evidence cleanup. This is the highest-fidelity detection opportunity in the entire kill chain — StopLogging fires a CloudWatch alarm before the API call completes.

implementation.method: boto3 CloudTrail and S3 calls using victim_creds. boto3 CodeCommit call using codecommit_role_session for repo evidence cleanup.

api_calls.note: Deletes most recent log file only. Full AMBERSQUID behavior deletes all logs. Lab: limit to 1 object.
Enumerates repos as precursor to deletion. Demonstrates AWSCodeCommit-Role used in T1070 phase per TI credential_chain.

detection_sources: CloudTrail management events (captured before StopLogging takes effect)
cleanup_actions: cloudtrail:StartLogging ambersquid-cloudtrail — re-enable IMMEDIATELY after test completes
S3 bucket versioning is enabled on ambersquid-cloudtrail-bucket — deleted objects recoverable via S3 version restoration
iocs_generated: CloudTrail StopLogging event — highest-fidelity detection signal in entire kill chain
S3 DeleteObject on CloudTrail log prefix
GuardDuty finding: Stealth:IAMUser/CloudTrailLoggingDisabled
CloudWatch Alarm trigger: CloudTrailLoggingDisabled

attack_chain.description: Attacker's containers execute SRBMiner-MULTI targeting ZEPHYR, TRX, RVN, and XMR mining pools via configured wallet addresses. Simulated — the ECS task running from Step 3 uses ubuntu:22.04 with 'sleep infinity'. No real miner binary, no mining pool connections. All known mining pool ports (3333, 4444, 5555, 7777, 8888, 9999, 14444, 45560) are blocked by ambersquid-task-sg.

implementation.method: Verify the ECS task from Step 3 is still running. Log mock mining output. No additional API calls launch new compute.

api_calls.note: Confirms task still running — no additional launch needed. Container running 'sleep infinity' is the benign miner stand-in.

implementation.simulation_note: SIMULATED: attack.py logs 'SIMULATED [T1496]: SRBMiner-MULTI would connect to 2miners/c3pool/nanopool on ports 3333/4444/5555. Wallet addresses: ZEPHYR2vyrpcg2e2sJaA88EM6aGaLCBdiYfiHffrs5b3Fa4p1qpoEPH4UabmhJr5YYF7CxJykLTJmESQWaB9ARNuhb6jvptapVq3v (ZEPHYR), TFrQ7u9spKk8MBgX6Bze3oxPbs3Yh1tAsq (TRX), and others. Mining pool ports blocked by ambersquid-task-sg. Mock miner exits immediately.'

credential_chain.source: Victim AWS credentials (AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY) injected as plaintext environment variables into ambersquid-task-definition ECS container. Harvested by attacker's container entrypoint.sh on startup.
credential_chain.expiration: long-lived — IAM user static key, never expires unless rotated by victim

credential_chain.source: STS AssumeRole on AWSCodeCommit-Role created in T1136.003 (step 5) using victim_creds. Session tokens obtained in T1098.001 (step 6).
STS AssumeRole on sugo-role created in T1136.003 (step 5) using victim_creds. Session tokens obtained in T1098.001 (step 6).
STS AssumeRole on ecsTaskExecutionRole (attacker-created with AdministratorAccess) created in T1136.003 (step 5) using victim_creds. Session tokens obtained in T1098.001 (step 6).

script_manifest.description: Single unified Python attack script managing credential chain across all phases. Reads victim credentials from environment variables AWS_VICTIM_ACCESS_KEY_ID and AWS_VICTIM_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY (or Pulumi stack output). Constructs victim_creds boto3 session, then assumes AWSCodeCommit-Role, sugo-role, and ecsTaskExecutionRole to build three parallel role sessions. Executes all 13 kill chain steps in TI order with configurable inter-step delays. Simulated steps log 'SIMULATED [TXXXX]:' prefix and execute only safe dry-run API calls.

phases.name: Persistence — IAM Role Creation and Session Establishment

attack_created_resources.name: test in us-west-2 (if successfully created in Step 7)
ambersquid-cloudtrail (stopped — must be re-enabled before pulumi destroy)
CloudTrail log objects deleted from ambersquid-cloudtrail-bucket (S3 versioning enabled — restorable)

cleanup_manifest.pulumi_managed_resources: All resources in infra_plan.json destroyed by pulumi destroy — including ambersquid-codecommit-repo (us-east-1), ambersquid-cloudtrail, ambersquid-cloudtrail-bucket, ambersquid-terraform-state-bucket, ambersquid-canary-secret, ambersquid-honey-iam-user, ambersquid-ecs-cluster, ambersquid-task-definition, ambersquid-vpc and all networking.
cleanup_order: 1. Re-enable CloudTrail: cloudtrail:StartLogging ambersquid-cloudtrail — do this FIRST before any other cleanup
2. Stop running ECS tasks: ecs:StopTask on all tasks in ambersquid-ecs-cluster
3. Stop SageMaker notebook: sagemaker:StopNotebookInstance miner-notebook — wait for Stopped state (may take 2-3 minutes)
7. Deregister ECS task definitions: ecs:DeregisterTaskDefinition miner-task and miner-fargate-task all revisions
9. Detach all policies then delete IAM roles: AWSCodeCommit-Role, sugo-role, ecsTaskExecutionRole (attacker-created)
10. Delete CodeCommit repo in us-west-2 if created: codecommit:DeleteRepository test
11. pulumi destroy — destroys all Pulumi-managed resources
